Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "dashboard#index"

  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"

  resource :session
  resources :passwords, param: :token

  get  "invites/:token",        to: "registrations#new",    as: :new_registration
  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password
    patch :toggle_tailscale
    post :generate_token
    delete "revoke_token/:id", action: :revoke_token, as: :revoke_token
  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify
  get  "auth/device/approve/:id",  to: "device_auth#confirm",  as: :auth_device_confirm
  post "auth/device/approve",      to: "device_auth#approve",  as: :auth_device_approve

  resources :sandboxes, only: [ :new, :create, :show, :destroy ] do
    member do
      post :start
      post :stop
      post :retry
      get :stats, controller: "dashboard", action: "stats"
      get :card, controller: "dashboard", action: "card"
      post :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "open"
      get  "terminal/wait", controller: "terminal", action: "wait"
      get  "terminal/status", controller: "terminal", action: "status"
      delete :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "close"
      post :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "open"
      get  "vnc/wait", controller: "vnc", action: "wait", as: :vnc_wait
      get  "vnc/status", controller: "vnc", action: "status", as: :vnc_status
      delete :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "close"
      post :snapshot, controller: "snapshots", action: "create_for_sandbox"
    end
  end

  resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :destroy ], param: :name do
    member do
      post :clone
    end
  end

  get "terminal/auth", to: "terminal#auth"
  get "vnc/auth", to: "vnc#auth"

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
    get :show
    post :login
    get :login_status
    get :connected
    patch :update_settings
    delete :disable
  end

  namespace :admin do
    get "/", to: "dashboard#index", as: :dashboard
    get "system_status", to: "dashboard#system_status"
    resource :settings, only: [ :edit, :update ]
    resources :users
    resources :invites, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    resources :sandboxes, only: :destroy do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        get :stats
      end
    end

    # Job monitoring dashboard
    mount MissionControl::Jobs::Engine, at: "/jobs"

    # Error tracking dashboard
    mount SolidErrors::Engine, at: "/errors"
  end

  namespace :api do
    resources :sandboxes do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        post :connect
        post :snapshot
        post :restore
        post :tailscale_connect
        delete :tailscale_disconnect
      end
      resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ], param: :domain, constraints: { domain: %r{[^/]+} }
    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code
      post :device_token
    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
      post :enable
      post :login
      get :login_status
      patch :update_settings
      delete :disable
      get :status
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

file.content:
class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    manager = TerminalManager.new

    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)

    # ttyd starts inside the container at boot; probe and redirect immediately
    # if it's already listening, otherwise send to wait page.
    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
      redirect_to terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: type), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/#{@type}")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/(tmux|shell)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user    = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.thinking:
The URL is `/terminal/9/tmux` - this is likely being served by Traefik as a reverse proxy to a WeTTY container or ttyd. The controller references `TerminalManager` and talks about ttyd starting inside the container. Let me look at the TerminalManager service to understand the flow.

The 404 suggests the route `/terminal/9/tmux` is expected to be served by Traefik but something isn't set up. Let me check the TerminalManager.

1→class TerminalManager
     2→  DATA_DIR    = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     4→  DYNAMIC_DIR  = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
     5→
     6→  TMUX_PORT  = 7681
     7→  SHELL_PORT = 7682
     8→
     9→  class Error < StandardError; end
    10→
    11→  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
    12→  # type: "tmux" (default) or "shell"
    13→  # Writes Traefik config (idempotent) and returns the terminal URL.
    14→  def open(sandbox:, type: "tmux")
    15→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    16→
    17→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    18→    terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    19→  end
    20→
    21→  # Returns true if ttyd is listening on the appropriate port inside the sandbox container.
    22→  # Uses docker exec + ss to check from inside the container — works in both dev (host Rails)
    23→  # and production (Rails in Docker) without needing Docker network hostname resolution.
    24→  def active?(sandbox:, type: "tmux")
    25→    return false if sandbox.container_id.blank?
    26→
    27→    port = type == "shell" ? SHELL_PORT : TMUX_PORT
    28→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    29→    _out, _err, code = container.exec([ "sh", "-c", "ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep -q ':#{port}' || netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep -q ':#{port}'" ])
    30→    code == 0
    31→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    32→    Rails.logger.debug("TerminalManager#active? #{sandbox.full_name}:#{port} → #{e.class}: #{e.message}")
    33→    false
    34→  end
    35→
    36→  # Deletes the Traefik config for the given sandbox.
    37→  def close(sandbox:)
    38→    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    39→  end
    40→
    41→  # Scans terminal-*.yml files; removes configs for sandboxes that no longer exist or aren't running.
    42→  def cleanup_orphaned
    43→    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-*.yml")).each do |path|
    44→      match = File.basename(path).match(/\Aterminal-(\d+)\.yml\z/)
    45→      next unless match
    46→
    47→      sandbox_id = match[1].to_i
    48→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id)
    49→
    50→      if sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"
    51→        File.delete(path)
    52→        Rails.logger.info("TerminalManager: removed orphaned Traefik config #{File.basename(path)}")
    53→      end
    54→    end
    55→  end
    56→
    57→  private
    58→
    59→  def terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    60→    "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"
    61→  end
    62→
    63→  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    64→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    65→    config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    66→    return if File.exist?(config_path)
    67→
    68→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    69→    id   = sandbox.id
    70→    container = sandbox.full_name
    71→
    72→    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
    73→      tmux_rule  = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    74→      shell_rule = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    75→    else
    76→      tmux_rule  = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    77→      shell_rule = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    78→    end
    79→
    80→    config = {
    81→      "http" => {
    82→        "routers" => {

    84→            "rule"        => tmux_rule,
    85→            "service"     => "terminal-#{id}-tmux",
    86→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
    87→            "tls"         => tls_config,
    88→            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}", "terminal-#{id}-strip-tmux" ],

   133→    case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
   134→    when "selfsigned", "mkcert"
   135→      {}
   136→    else
   137→      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
   138→    end
   139→  end
   140→
   141→  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
   142→    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
   143→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   144→  end
   145→end

file.content:
# Build websockify-go: minimal WebSocket→TCP proxy (~5 MB static binary)
FROM golang:1.23-alpine AS websockify-builder
WORKDIR /build
COPY websockify/ .
# go mod tidy downloads deps and generates go.sum; -mod=mod allows updating go.sum at build time
RUN go mod tidy && CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o websockify .

FROM ubuntu:25.10

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    openssh-server sudo curl git tmux vim neovim \
    build-essential \
    jq ripgrep fd-find htop wget unzip ca-certificates net-tools iproute2 iputils-ping \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GUI tools: TigerVNC (Xvnc = virtual X server + VNC server combined) and window manager.
# Xvnc sends the RFB banner immediately on connect, unlike x11vnc 0.9.17+ which waits
# for client data first (breaking websockify's server-speaks-first expectation).
# noVNC static files are served from the Rails app (public/novnc/), not from the sandbox.
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    tigervnc-standalone-server openbox xterm xfonts-base xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# websockify-go: single static binary replaces python3-websockify (~50 MB → ~5 MB)
COPY --from=websockify-builder /build/websockify /usr/local/bin/websockify

# Google Chrome (amd64) or Chromium (arm64) — Google doesn't ship a Chrome deb for arm64
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "amd64" ]; then \
      apt-get update \
      && wget -q https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && apt-get install -y ./google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    else \
      # Ubuntu ships chromium as a snap-only wrapper since 19.10 — unusable in containers.
      # Pull the real chromium .deb from Debian testing (arm64 only), pinned so nothing
      # else is silently upgraded from Debian.
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl gpg \
      && curl -fsSL https://ftp-master.debian.org/keys/archive-key-12.asc \
           | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/debian-archive.gpg \
      && echo "deb [arch=arm64] http://deb.debian.org/debian testing main" \
           > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/debian-testing.list \
      && printf 'Package: *\nPin: release o=Debian\nPin-Priority: 100\n\nPackage: chromium chromium-common chromium-sandbox\nPin: release o=Debian\nPin-Priority: 500\n' \
           > /etc/apt/preferences.d/debian-chromium \
      && apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    fi

# Prefer IPv4 to avoid slow/broken IPv6 connections
RUN sed -i 's/#precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/' /etc/gai.conf

# Docker CLI + daemon (Sysbox makes this safe)
# Use manual apt repo instead of get.docker.com convenience script —
# that script tries to install ca-certificates/curl which Ubuntu 25.10 already
# has at newer versions, causing "E: Packages were downgraded".
# Pin to 'noble' — Docker has no packages for Ubuntu 25.10 (questing) yet.
RUN install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu noble stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \
    && apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Pin runc to 1.1.x — runc 1.2+ added a procfs safety check that fails
# inside sysbox containers (sysbox-fs mounts /proc/sys as separate FUSE)
RUN RUNC_VERSION="v1.1.15" \
    && ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/${RUNC_VERSION}/runc.${ARCH}" \
       -o /usr/bin/runc \
    && chmod +x /usr/bin/runc

# Docker Compose plugin
RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/latest/download/docker-compose-linux-$(uname -m)" \
       -o /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose

# Stage mise + Claude Code in /opt/sandcastle/bin (seeded to ~/.local/bin on first boot)
RUN mkdir -p /opt/sandcastle/bin \
    && curl https://mise.run | REDACTED sh \
    && curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash \
    && cp -L /root/.local/bin/claude /opt/sandcastle/bin/claude \
    && rm -rf /root/.local/share/claude /root/.local/bin/claude

# ttyd — web terminal (two ports: 7681=tmux, 7682=shell)
RUN ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
    && TTYD_ARCH=$([ "$ARCH" = "arm64" ] && echo "aarch64" || echo "x86_64") \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/latest/download/ttyd.${TTYD_ARCH}" \
       -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd

# Entire CLI — git observability for AI agent sessions (https://entire.io)
RUN ARCH="$(dpkg --print-architecture)" \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/entireio/cli/releases/latest/download/entire_linux_${ARCH}.tar.gz" \
       | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin entire \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entire

# Shell config: PATH, mise activation, aliases
RUN echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo 'eval "$(mise activate bash)"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo "alias yolo='claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# SSH configuration (key-only auth) — use drop-in config for Ubuntu 25.10 compatibility
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d \
    && echo "PermitRootLogin no" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PasswordAuthentication no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -s /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

WORKDIR /workspace
EXPOSE 22 5900 6080 7681 7682

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→set -e
     3→
     4→USERNAME="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandbox}"
     5→SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY:-}"
     6→
     7→# Create user if not exists

#!/bin/bash
set -e

USERNAME="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandbox}"
SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY:-}"

# Create user if not exists
if ! id "$USERNAME" &>/dev/null; then
    useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo,docker "$USERNAME"
    echo "$USERNAME ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
    chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
fi

# Set up SSH authorized keys (append if not already present, preserving
# any WeTTY keys that may have been injected for other sandboxes sharing
# this user's home directory via bind mount).
if [ -n "$SSH_KEY" ]; then
    SSH_DIR="/home/$USERNAME/.ssh"
    mkdir -p "$SSH_DIR"
    if [ -f "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys" ]; then
        grep -qF "$SSH_KEY" "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys" || echo "$SSH_KEY" >> "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    else
        echo "$SSH_KEY" > "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    fi
    chmod 700 "$SSH_DIR"
    chmod 600 "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "$SSH_DIR"
fi

# Ensure home directory ownership and permissions.
# The host may create bind-mounted home dirs with 777 so Sysbox-mapped root
# can write. Tighten to 755 here so sshd StrictModes is satisfied.
chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME"
chmod 755 "/home/$USERNAME"

# Ensure workspace is accessible
chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /workspace 2>/dev/null || true

# Seed mise + Claude Code into user's ~/.local/bin on first boot
USER_LOCAL_BIN="/home/$USERNAME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$USER_LOCAL_BIN"
for tool in mise claude; do
    if [ ! -f "$USER_LOCAL_BIN/$tool" ] && [ -f "/opt/sandcastle/bin/$tool" ]; then
        cp "/opt/sandcastle/bin/$tool" "$USER_LOCAL_BIN/$tool"
    fi
done
chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME/.local"

# Configure git identity system-wide if provided
if [ -n "$USER_FULLNAME" ] || [ -n "$USER_EMAIL" ]; then
    {
        echo "[user]"
        [ -n "$USER_FULLNAME" ] && echo "    name = $USER_FULLNAME"
        [ -n "$USER_EMAIL" ] && echo "    email = $USER_EMAIL"
    } >> /etc/gitconfig
fi

# Generate SSH host keys if missing
ssh-keygen -A

# Resize /dev/shm to 2GB for Chrome. Docker's ShmSize HostConfig key is not
# supported by sysbox-runc, so we do it here instead. The runtime bind-mounts
# /dev/shm from a small constrained shm, so "remount,size=" fails; unmounting
# it and mounting a fresh tmpfs works.
umount /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true
mount -t tmpfs -o size=2g,mode=1777 tmpfs /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true

# Start Docker daemon in background (requires Sysbox runtime for isolated /var/lib/docker)
# Don't wait for it to be ready - users can check with `docker info` after SSH login
if command -v dockerd &>/dev/null && [ -e /dev/fuse ]; then
    # Match inner Docker bridge MTU to container's eth0 to avoid packet fragmentation
    ETH0_MTU=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \K[0-9]+' || echo 1500)
    dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu="$ETH0_MTU" &>/var/log/dockerd.log &
else
    echo "Note: Docker-in-Docker not available (requires sysbox-runc runtime)" >&2
fi

# Start virtual X + VNC server for browser access.
# Xvnc (TigerVNC) combines Xvfb and a VNC server in a single process and sends
# the RFB banner immediately on connect — required for websockify compatibility.
# x11vnc 0.9.17+ waits for client data before sending the banner, deadlocking
# with websockify which also waits for the server to speak first.
# Both processes run as $USERNAME (not root) for proper display ownership.
VNC_ENABLED="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED:-1}"
VNC_GEOMETRY="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY:-1280x900}"
VNC_DEPTH="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH:-24}"

if command -v Xvnc &>/dev/null && [ "$VNC_ENABLED" = "1" ]; then
    touch /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
        "Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry ${VNC_GEOMETRY} -depth ${VNC_DEPTH} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &"
    # Start Openbox window manager once the display is ready
    if command -v openbox &>/dev/null; then
        su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
            'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &'
    fi
    # Start websockify-go: proxies WebSocket connections to Xvnc on port 6080.
    # noVNC static files are served from the Rails app (public/novnc/), not here.
    touch /var/log/websockify.log
    chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /var/log/websockify.log
    websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log &
    # Export DISPLAY for all SSH sessions via PAM environment
    echo "DISPLAY=:99" >> /etc/environment
fi

# Start ttyd web terminals (run as sandbox user)
# Port 7681: tmux session (persistent, re-attaches; unlimited clients)
# Port 7682: plain login shell (one client at a time)
if command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
    touch /var/log/ttyd-tmux.log /var/log/ttyd-shell.log
    chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /var/log/ttyd-tmux.log /var/log/ttyd-shell.log
    su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
        "ttyd -W -m 0 -p 7681 tmux new-session -A -s main &>/var/log/ttyd-tmux.log &"
    su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
        "ttyd -W -m 1 -p 7682 bash -l &>/var/log/ttyd-shell.log &"
fi

# Start SSH daemon in foreground
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D -e

content.thinking:
The architecture is now ttyd-based - ttyd runs inside the sandbox container at ports 7681 (tmux) and 7682 (shell). Traefik routes `/terminal/{id}/tmux` to the sandbox container at that port.

The 404 at `/terminal/9/tmux` likely means:
1. Traefik config for terminal-9 doesn't exist, OR
2. Traefik config exists but is routing to a container name that doesn't match, OR
3. Something about the routing itself

The controller `open` action:
1. Calls `manager.open(sandbox:, type:)` → writes Traefik config


2. Checks `manager.active?` (ss/netstat inside container) → if ready, redirects to `/terminal/9/tmux`
3. If not ready → redirects to wait page which polls status

So a 404 on `/terminal/9/tmux` is Traefik saying it doesn't know this route. This means either the Traefik config file wasn't written, or the sandbox got restarted and the config was deleted. Looking at the `write_traefik_config` method...

Actually, wait—the user mentioned "stuff got lost on merge," which suggests this is a code issue rather than a runtime problem. Let me trace through the recent commits to see what changed. The entrypoint comment still references something that might've been removed during the merge.

The real question is whether the Traefik dynamic config for that terminal instance is being generated correctly, or if the sandbox isn't on the right Docker network, or if something in the code broke. I need to check what `sandbox.full_name` resolves to and whether the Traefik service URL would be constructed properly.

class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy

  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") } },
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command
    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

  # Job lifecycle management
  def job_in_progress?
    job_status.present?
  end

  def job_failed?
    job_error.present?
  end

  def start_job(status)
    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
  end

  def finish_job
    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  def fail_job(error_message)
    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  private

  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    broadcast_prepend_to(
      [user, "dashboard"],
      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
      locals: { sandbox: self },
      target: "sandboxes"
    )
  end

  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    # If sandbox is destroyed, remove it from the dashboard instead of replacing
    if status == "destroyed"
      broadcast_remove_to([user, "dashboard"], target: dom_id(self))
    else
      broadcast_replace_to(
        [user, "dashboard"],
        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
        locals: { sandbox: self },
        target: dom_id(self)
      )
    end
  end

  def broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    broadcast_remove_to([user, "dashboard"], target: dom_id(self))
  end

end

Let me check SandboxManager to understand how containers are named and connected to the network.

content.thinking:
The container is named `sandbox.full_name` (e.g., `username-sandbox_name`) and is on the `sandcastle-web` network. The Traefik service URL is `http://#{container}:#{TMUX_PORT}` = `http://username-sandbox_name:7681`.

Since this is a dev environment (`dev.sand:8443`), let me think about what might cause a 404. 

The `dev.sand:8443` suggests it's running behind a Traefik with selfsigned TLS mode. The 404 from Traefik means it has no matching route for `/terminal/9/tmux`.
 
Looking at the recent commits, the ttyd change was introduced in `4e5a4e9` and fixed in `91876a1`, with HEAD currently at `a13aa9c`. The flow is: clicking "open terminal" triggers the Rails controller, which writes a dynamic Traefik config file for that terminal, then Traefik routes the subsequent request to the sandbox container. Something in that chain broke during the merge.

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = VncManager.new

    # Already up? Skip the wait page entirely.
    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to vnc_redirect_url(manager.vnc_url(sandbox)), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
      return
    end

    # Write Traefik config now (web container has correct SANDCASTLE_HOST).
    # The job runs in the worker which may lack this env var.
    manager.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)

    # Kick off container setup in background and return immediately.
    VncOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url(VncManager.new.vnc_url(@sandbox))
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

**VNC `open` action:**

2. Call `prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)` (writes config)

4. If not → redirect to wait page

The terminal doesn't use a background job because ttyd starts inside the container automatically via entrypoint.sh, so no separate setup is needed. VNC uses a background job though, probably for some Docker network setup or similar configuration that happens asynchronously.

file.content:
class VncOpenJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return unless sandbox.status == "running"

    sandbox.start_job("opening_vnc")

    begin
      VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
      sandbox.finish_job
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("VncOpenJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to open browser: #{e.message}")
      # Don't re-raise - VNC failures shouldn't retry
    end
  end
end

That said, the real issue is likely that the terminal was never opened through the Rails UI after the ttyd migration, so the config file was never written in the first place, or it got lost during a container restart.

The user needs to click "Open Terminal" from the sandbox UI to trigger the `POST /sandboxes/9/terminal` endpoint, which writes the Traefik config. But they mentioned "stuff got lost on merge" — maybe something was accidentally deleted during a recent merge. Let me check the git history to see if there are any suspicious commits.

Looking at the commit log, I can see the ttyd migration replaced WeTTY entirely. The terminal controller used to handle WeTTY sidecars and SSH keys, but now it's much simpler. The 404 error would happen if either the sandbox image is outdated and doesn't have ttyd installed yet, or if they're running old sandbox containers created before ttyd was added to the Dockerfile.

The user seems to be asking whether something got lost in the merge that caused this 404. Now I'm looking at how TerminalManager writes the Traefik configuration with the stripPrefix middlewares, and the 404 suggests Traefik isn't finding a matching route. Let me trace through the `active?` method to see if there's an issue with how it checks whether tmux or shell is actually running inside the container—it's checking for open ports using `ss` or `netstat`, but the logic might be incomplete here. the container boots ttyd, which listens on a port inside the sandbox. The open action writes the Traefik config, checks if the terminal manager is active, and either redirects immediately to the terminal or sends the user to a wait page that polls for readiness. Once ttyd starts listening, the status endpoint returns "ready" and the frontend redirects to the actual terminal URL.

The 404 is probably a runtime issue—either the Traefik config file was never created because the terminal wasn't opened through the UI, the sandbox image is outdated and doesn't have ttyd, or there's a mismatch between where Traefik is looking for dynamic configs in development. I should check if any view templates for the terminal got removed during the merge.

The code looks complete. Let me now think about what's actually causing the 404.

Looking at the terminal architecture:
1. User clicks "Open Terminal" → POST to `/sandboxes/9/terminal` → `TerminalController#open`
2. `manager.open()` writes `terminal-9.yml` to `DYNAMIC_DIR`
3. If ttyd is already listening (active?), redirect to `/terminal/9/tmux`
4. Else, redirect to wait page which polls until ttyd is ready

The Traefik dynamic config at `/terminal/9/tmux` should only be reached after clicking the terminal button. 

But I'm realizing the documentation in CLAUDE.md describes the old WeTTY flow, not the current ttyd setup. The issue is that even though `manager.open()` writes the Traefik config early with a 1-second sleep to let it load, there's a race condition: if `active?` detects ttyd is running and redirects immediately, Traefik might not have picked up the new route yet, causing the 404.

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`sandcastle.tc`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
    volumes:
      - .:/rails
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  bundle-data:
  node-modules:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

content.thinking:
Now I can see the dev setup. Notice:

1. `SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc` but the user is accessing `dev.sand:8443`
2. `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443`
3. `SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned`

The Traefik config uses `HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(...)` in selfsigned mode (which matches any host), so the host mismatch between `sandcastle.tc` and `dev.sand` shouldn't matter for Traefik routing.

But here's the key issue: The `SANDCASTLE_HOST` is set to `sandcastle.tc` but the user is accessing `dev.sand:8443`. This is fine since selfsigned mode uses `HostRegexp`. But `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL` is set to `https://sandcastle.tc:8443`. So when the user clicks "Open Terminal", they'd be redirected to `https://sandcastle.tc: 8443/terminal/9/tmux` instead of staying on `dev.sand:8443` — that's likely causing the 404. The init-traefik config creates a route for `Host("sandcastle.tc")`, and the terminal route would need to be dynamically created via `POST /sandboxes/9/terminal`, but the web service is using `dev.sand` which doesn't match the Traefik host rule.

Actually, stepping back — the user is saying they got a 404 when accessing the terminal and wondering if something broke in a recent merge. The real issue is probably that the Traefik config file for that sandbox (`terminal-9.yml`) either doesn't exist in the volume or the sandbox container isn't reachable from Traefik. The code structure looks correct, but there's a guard checking if the config file already exists before writing it. The 404 likely happens because the Traefik config file for that sandbox was never created—either the user navigated directly to the URL instead of clicking "Open Terminal" (which triggers the config creation), or the volume was reset. The proper flow is clicking "Open Terminal" to call the endpoint that writes the config, then the wait page polls for readiness and redirects. I should verify the `active?` method doesn't interfere with this redirect logic.

Actually, I'm realizing the most probable cause is that sandbox 9 is running an outdated image without ttyd installed, so the container never starts the terminal service and `active?` perpetually returns false. Let me check what the dev environment is actually using. I should check whether the sandbox image includes version information or a marker that indicates if ttyd was installed. The wait template redirects to `terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/#{@type}")`, which resolves based on `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL` — so if the user is accessing from `dev.sand:8443` but that environment variable points elsewhere, the redirect won't work. That's likely a dev setup issue rather than a code loss problem.

I'm realizing there's another scenario I hadn't fully considered. The Rails container and Traefik both mount the `traefik-data` volume, so when Rails writes the dynamic config file to `/data/traefik/dynamic/terminal-9.yml`, it appears at `/data/dynamic/terminal-9.yml` inside the Traefik container, which is watching that directory for configuration changes.

The host rule in the terminal router is quite broad with `HostRegexp(`.+`)`, so it should match any incoming host, and the priority of 100 should ensure it takes precedence over other routes. This all seems correct, so I'm ready to share my findings with the user.

content.text:
The code looks intact — nothing was lost in recent merges. Here's what's actually going on:

**The 404 is Traefik saying it has no dynamic config for `/terminal/9/tmux`.** That config file (`terminal-9.yml`) is written to the `traefik-data` volume only when you click "Open Terminal" (POST to `/sandboxes/9/terminal`). If you navigated to the URL directly, or the volume was reset, Traefik doesn't know the route exists.

The most likely culprits:

1. **Sandbox image is outdated** — If sandbox 9 was created before the WeTTY→ttyd migration (`4e5a4e9`), its container doesn't have `ttyd` installed. The entrypoint's ttyd block silently skips it (`command -v ttyd` fails). You'd need to destroy and recreate the sandbox.

2. **Traefik config was lost** — If the `traefik-data` volume was reset (e.g. `docker compose down -v`), all dynamic configs are gone. Just click "Open Terminal" again to recreate it.

3. **One minor stale thing I noticed** — `docker-compose.dev.yml` has `SANDCASTLE_HOST: sandcastle.tc` and `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://sandcastle.tc:8443`, but you're hitting `dev.sand:8443`. The selfsigned mode uses `HostRegexp(`.+`)` so Traefik routing isn't affected, but the wait page would redirect to `sandcastle.tc:8443/terminal/9/tmux` after polling. If you're using `dev.sand` as your local hostname, you'd want `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443` in your local env.

**Quick check:** click "Open Terminal" on the sandbox page (POST flow) — if ttyd is in the image, the Traefik config gets written and it should work.

content.text:
Done. Three places updated to `dev.sand`:
- `SANDCASTLE_HOST` (web + worker)
- `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL` + `SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL` (web)
- Traefik static Rails router rule (init-traefik)

You'll need to recreate the containers for it to take effect (`docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d`). The `traefik-data` volume will also be reinitialised with the new `rails.yml`.

content.thinking:
The user wants to set `dev.sand` in one place (like an env var or `.env` file) and reference it everywhere else in `docker-compose.dev.yml`. Docker Compose supports variable substitution from a `.env` file or environment. We can define something like `DEV_HOST=dev.sand` and use `${DEV_HOST}` throughout the compose file.

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`dev.sand`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`dev.sand`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://dev.sand:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
    volumes:
      - .:/rails
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  bundle-data:
  node-modules:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

content.thinking:
The challenge is that `dev.sand` appears in three ways:
1. In environment variables: `SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand`, `SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443`, `SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://dev.sand:8443` - these can use `${DEV_HOST:-dev.sand}` substitution
2. In the `init-traefik` shell command - this is a heredoc inside a shell command, which doesn't interpolate Docker Compose variables directly. But we can change the heredoc delimiter approach.

The issue with the `init-traefik` service is that it uses a heredoc with a quoted delimiter (`'EOFCONFIG'`), which prevents variable substitution in the shell. But Docker Compose DOES interpolate `${VAR}` in the `command` field BEFORE passing to the container. 

So the key insight is that Docker Compose substitutes variables first, then passes the result to the shell. If I change the heredoc from a quoted delimiter to an unquoted one, the shell will see the already-substituted value and won't try to expand it again—but I need to be careful about the order of operations here. prevents the shell from expanding variables inside the heredoc, so the literal string `${DEV_HOST}` gets written to the file—but Docker Compose already substituted it at the YAML level, so the file ends up with `dev.sand` instead.

Now I need to implement this: add `DEV_HOST=dev.sand` to the `.env` file and use `${DEV_HOST:-dev.sand}` as a fallback throughout the compose configuration. Let me check what's currently in those environment files.

content.thinking:
Good. The `.env` file already exists and is loaded by Docker Compose automatically. I'll add `DEV_HOST=dev.sand` to `.env` and then replace all occurrences in `docker-compose.dev.yml` with `${DEV_HOST}`.

The key insight: Docker Compose interpolates `${DEV_HOST}` from the `.env` file (or environment) in ALL YAML values, including inside shell command strings. So even in the `init-traefik` heredoc, `${DEV_HOST}` in the YAML string will be replaced before the shell receives it. The `'EOFCONFIG'` heredoc prevents SHELL expansion, but Docker Compose substitution happens at the YAML level beforehand.


Now I'm updating the `.env` and `docker-compose.dev.yml` files with these changes.

content.thinking:
The issue is clear: the browser is redirected to `https://dev.sand:8443/terminal/10/tmux`, but instead of Traefik catching it and routing to ttyd, the request is hitting Rails (which has no route for `/terminal/10/tmux`).

This means Traefik is NOT catching the `/terminal/10/tmux` request — it's falling through to Rails. 

Why? Because the `init-traefik` service runs ONCE and writes `rails.yml` statically. But the terminal Traefik config (`terminal-10.yml`) IS being written (the redirect happened, so `manager.open()` ran), but Traefik isn't routing it — instead Rails is seeing the GET request.
 
So Traefik is forwarding the request to Rails because the Rails router matches `Host("dev.sand")` on all requests to that host, while the terminal router has a more specific path prefix but maybe lacks the priority to override it. I need to check if the Rails config actually has a priority set. Looking at the terminal router configuration, it has an explicit priority of 100, which should take precedence over the Rails router's default priority. The real issue is that the POST request is getting a 303 redirect to the terminal path, but the GET request following that redirect is hitting Rails instead of the terminal router—suggesting either the `terminal-10.yml` file wasn't written in time, Traefik hasn't reloaded it yet, or Traefik is still choosing the Rails router despite the higher priority.

The fact that reloading the page works confirms this is purely a timing issue: the file gets written and Traefik eventually picks it up, but the redirect happens too quickly, before Traefik has loaded the new configuration. The `active?` check returns true after ~68ms (confirming ttyd is listening), but the redirect happens immediately without waiting for Traefik to actually start routing to the terminal.

Looking back at the history, there was a 1-second sleep that got removed for performance reasons, and that sleep was specifically there to give Traefik time to detect the new route. The real fix is to always redirect to the wait page first, which then polls the status endpoint until both ttyd is listening AND Traefik has the config loaded before doing the final redirect to the terminal.

The wait page currently only checks if ttyd is active, but it needs to also verify Traefik has loaded the configuration. The simplest approach is to always send users to the wait page after writing the config—it'll handle the polling and redirect to the actual terminal URL once everything is ready, avoiding the race condition that the removed sleep was protecting against.

Looking at the controller code...

Still writing controller logic... the real problem is that `write_traefik_config` finishes before Traefik actually processes the file, so even though `active?` confirms ttyd is running, we redirect too fast and hit stale config. The wait page's immediate `check()` call gives us a network round-trip delay that buys maybe 50-100ms, but that's unreliable—a better approach would be to always use the wait page with an intentional initial delay before polling, ensuring Traefik has time to catch up. Simplifying the open action to always redirect to the wait page, removing the fast path that checks if Traefik is already active—this way the polling logic handles both first-time and subsequent opens consistently without worrying about race conditions.

content.text:
The race condition: Rails writes `terminal-10.yml` then immediately redirects the browser to `/terminal/10/tmux`. Traefik's file watcher hasn't picked up the new config yet, so it falls through to the Rails router — which has no route for it. On reload, Traefik has loaded it and it works.

The fix: always go through the wait page. It polls for ttyd readiness (typically < 250ms), which gives Traefik enough time to load the file. The old "redirect immediately if already active" fast path is what causes the race.

class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    manager = TerminalManager.new

    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)

    # ttyd starts inside the container at boot; probe and redirect immediately
    # if it's already listening, otherwise send to wait page.
    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
      redirect_to terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: type), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/#{@type}")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/(tmux|shell)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user    = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = VncManager.new

    # Already up? Skip the wait page entirely.
    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to vnc_redirect_url(manager.vnc_url(sandbox)), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
      return
    end

    # Write Traefik config now (web container has correct SANDCASTLE_HOST).
    # The job runs in the worker which may lack this env var.
    manager.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)

    # Kick off container setup in background and return immediately.
    VncOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url(VncManager.new.vnc_url(@sandbox))
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.thinking:
The user wants to check if the Traefik route is already loaded before redirecting directly. This way we get the fast path back when the route is already active, and only fall back to the wait page when the route was just written (and Traefik hasn't loaded it yet).

How to check if Traefik has loaded a route? Traefik has an API (`/api/http/routers`) that lists all active routers. We can query it to check if our router is registered.

In the dev compose, Traefik doesn't expose the API publicly... let me check. The command in docker-compose.dev.yml doesn't include `--api.insecure=true` or similar. But from inside the Docker network, we might be able to hit Traefik's API.


Actually, there's a simpler approach: I can check whether the config file already existed before writing it. If `write_traefik_config` returns early because the file was already there, Traefik has already loaded the route and we can redirect immediately. If it just wrote a new file, we need to wait. I'll modify `write_traefik_config` to return a boolean indicating whether it actually wrote a new file, then use that to decide between redirecting directly or showing the wait page. For the terminal, `open` in TerminalManager returns the URL, but I need to know if the config was...

Adding a helper method to check if the config exists... I'm refining the API so `open` returns both the terminal URL and a boolean indicating whether the config was newly written, letting the controller decide whether to redirect immediately or wait for the sandbox to be ready.

content.text: Yes — the config file is the source of truth. If it already existed before `open` was called, Traefik already has the route loaded and we can redirect directly. Only when we just wrote a new file do we need the wait page.

# Returns [url, newly_written] — newly_written is true when the Traefik
  # config was just created (Traefik hasn't loaded it yet), false when it
  # already existed (Traefik already has the route active).

class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    manager = TerminalManager.new

    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)

    # Always go through the wait page so Traefik has time to pick up the
    # newly written dynamic config before the browser hits /terminal/:id/:type.
    redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: type), status: :see_other
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/#{@type}")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/(tmux|shell)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user    = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = VncManager.new

    # Write Traefik config now (web container has correct SANDCASTLE_HOST).
    # The job runs in the worker which may lack this env var.
    manager.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)

    # Kick off container setup in background and return immediately.
    VncOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url(VncManager.new.vnc_url(@sandbox))
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.text: Yes. Traefik has an API we can query. The fix: enable the Traefik API in the compose file, then check it from the `status` action (what the wait page polls) — return "ready" only when both ttyd is up AND Traefik has the route loaded.

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6.8
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - /data/traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml:ro
      - /data/traefik/dynamic:/data/dynamic:ro
      - /data/traefik/acme.json:/data/acme.json
      - /data/traefik/certs:/data/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  web:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-988}"
    cap_add:
      - SYS_ADMIN
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /data:/data
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${SANDCASTLE_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: ${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-letsencrypt}
      SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES: ${SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES:-}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      GITHUB_CLIENT_ID: ${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}
      GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET: ${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
      GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID: ${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}
      GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET: ${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  worker:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["./bin/jobs"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-988}"
    cap_add:
      - SYS_ADMIN
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /data:/data
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${SANDCASTLE_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: ${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-letsencrypt}
      SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES: ${SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES:-}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  migrate:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY}
      AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT: ${AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

content.thinking:
Good. All three traefik.yml blocks have `api:` and `dashboard: false` already. I need to add `insecure: true` to each. The Traefik API is already enabled (because of `api:` being there), just not set to insecure mode. Wait, actually `api:` without `insecure: true` means the API is accessible only on the Traefik dashboard entry point, not on the main port. Let me check how Traefik API works.

In Traefik v3, when `api:` is set:
- The API is accessible via the "traefik" internal service  


- With `api.insecure: true`, it's exposed on port 8080 (a special entry point)
- Without `insecure: true`, you need to configure a router pointing to the `api@internal` service

So if `api.insecure: true` is NOT set, the API is only accessible if you've set up a router to `api@internal`. Currently the config doesn't have that, so the API is effectively disabled. I need to add `insecure: true` to the `api:` section in all three traefik.yml blocks in `installer/installer.sh.in` and the dev docker-compose.dev.

Now I'm setting up the Traefik API check in Rails—I can query `http://traefik:8080/api/http/routers/{name}@file` to verify routes are loaded. I'll add this check to the `status` actions in both controllers and create a `traefik_ready?` method for the manager services. For the router names in Traefik's file provider, I'm using the pattern `{router-name}@file` like `terminal-10-tmux@file` and `vnc-10@file`. I'll configure the Traefik API URL as an environment variable with a fallback to `http://traefik:8080`.

Now I'm implementing the changes across the codebase: enabling insecure mode in the Traefik configuration files, adding the `traefik_ready?` method to both manager services, integrating the Traefik readiness check into the controller status actions, and updating the necessary configuration files.

2. Add `traefik_ready?` to both managers
3. Gate the `status` action on both ttyd/websockify AND Traefik confirming the route
